libraries.name: Data & Table Toolkit
libraries.description: Prompts for converting raw data into clean tables, JSON, comparison grids, and normalized formats.

prompts.title: Create Table
prompts.prompt:
Create a clear, well-formatted markdown table from the data I provide.
- Use the first row as column headers (clean them up for readability if needed).
- Ensure all rows are aligned under the correct headers.
- Do not change any values, only structure them into a table.
- If the data looks inconsistent or ambiguous, briefly note any assumptions you make.

prompts.title: Clean List
prompts.prompt: Convert the data I provide into a clean, normalized list of unique items, removing duplicates and obvious typos while preserving the original meaning. Briefly mention any normalization rules you applied.

prompts.title: Tag Groups
prompts.prompt: From the data I provide, extract only the entries that match clearly defined categories or tags. Group the results by category, and list each group with a short one-sentence description of what it represents.

prompts.title: JSON Map
prompts.prompt: Turn the data I provide into a structured JSON object with clear, descriptive keys. Keep all values intact, infer reasonable field names where missing, and validate that the structure is consistent across all items.

prompts.title: Compare Grid
prompts.prompt: Convert the data I provide into a comparison matrix. Use rows for items and columns for their main attributes. Keep attribute names short and clear, and leave cells blank rather than guessing missing values.

prompts.title: Value Normalize
prompts.prompt: Given the data I provide, extract all date-, time-, or number-like values and standardize them into a consistent format (e.g., ISO dates, plain numbers). Return them in a structured list, and note any ambiguous entries you could not safely convert.

prompts.title: Trend Bullets
prompts.prompt: Summarize the key insights from the data I provide in 3–5 bullet points, focusing only on the most important trends, outliers, and patterns. Do not invent any numbers or facts that aren't explicitly present.

prompts.title: Data Audit
prompts.prompt: Analyze the data I provide and identify any inconsistencies, missing values, or formatting issues. Return a short report that lists problems, gives 1–2 concrete examples for each, and suggests how to clean them.

prompts.title: Exec Brief
prompts.prompt: From the data I provide, generate a concise executive summary (maximum 150 words) that explains what the data is about, who it is for, and what decisions it could help support.

libraries.name: Notion Page Formatter
libraries.description: 23 ready-to-paste prompts that transform messy text into structured Notion pages.

prompts.title: Dashboard Layout
prompts.prompt:
Reformat this page into a clean Notion dashboard.
- Start with a high-level overview section (summary, quick stats, or links).
- Group related info into clear H2 sections for easy scanning.
- Add callout blocks for key highlights:
✅ Wins or important metrics
ℹ️ Extra context or tips
⚠️ Blockers or issues
❗ Urgent priorities
- Collapse secondary details inside toggles.
- Create a resources/links section if needed.
- End with a Next Steps section summarizing key actions.

prompts.title: Notes & Knowledge Page
prompts.prompt:
Turn this raw text into a structured notes page.
- Summarize long sections into concise bullet points.
- Organize information into a clear hierarchy using proper headings.
- Move detailed explanations into toggles to reduce clutter.
- Highlight key terms or insights in callout blocks.
- Add a quick reference list or table of contents at the top.

prompts.title: Knowledge Wiki
prompts.prompt:
Reformat this into a wiki-style knowledge page.
- Organize content into H1/H2 sections by category.
- Use columns for related short info (e.g., quick facts, definitions).
- Highlight key terms or definitions in callouts.
- Add a Resources & Links section at the end.
- Include a mini table of contents at the top.

prompts.title: Study Guide
prompts.prompt:
Transform these notes into a comprehensive study guide.
- Begin with a concise summary of the key concepts.
- Create H2 sections for major topics or chapters.
- Add bullet point lists for important facts, formulas, and definitions.
- Include callout blocks for critical concepts that need emphasis.
- Create toggle blocks for detailed explanations or examples.
- Add a review questions section at the end.
- End with quick reference charts or memory aids if applicable.

prompts.title: Project Tracker
prompts.prompt:
Reorganize this into a project tracking hub.
- Identify and group projects, tasks, and milestones.
- Extract action items into a checklist with ✅ for completed, ⏳ for pending, ❗ for urgent.
- Organize dates & timelines in a clear sequence.
- Create a Project Overview section summarizing status, owners, and next steps.

prompts.title: Meeting Summary
prompts.prompt:
Reformat this messy meeting text into a structured summary.
- Add a header (Meeting Title, Date, Participants).
- Write a 2–3 sentence meeting summary.
- Group discussion points by topic or team.
- Pull out decisions made into a separate section.
- Extract action items into a checklist with owners if mentioned.
- Highlight important follow-ups in callout blocks.

prompts.title: Task Planner
prompts.prompt:
Convert this text into an organized task planner.
- Extract tasks into a priority checklist:
✅ Done
⏳ In progress
⚠️ Needs attention
- Group tasks into categories or timelines (Today, This Week, Later).
- Add a Goals section for big-picture context.
- Suggest a Kanban view structure if helpful.

prompts.title: Decision Log
prompts.prompt:
Convert this messy discussion into a decision log page.
- Start with a context summary (why this decision was needed).
- List options considered in bullet points.
- Highlight the final decision in a ✅ callout.
- Add any rationale or supporting notes in toggles.
- End with follow-up actions or next steps.

prompts.title: Research Brief
prompts.prompt:
Clean up this research dump into a structured research brief.
- Summarize key findings at the top.
- Create Pros/Cons lists where applicable.
- Use callouts for important stats, quotes, or insights.
- Group references or sources in a Resources section.
- End with recommended next steps based on findings.

prompts.title: Content Outline
prompts.prompt:
Turn these scattered notes into a clear content outline.
- Identify the main sections (H1).
- Nest subtopics (H2/H3) under each section.
- Add notes on tone, style, and key messages.
- Suggest a logical order for storytelling.
- End with a CTA or final takeaway section if needed.

prompts.title: Resource Library
prompts.prompt:
Reformat this into a resource library page.
- Categorize resources by type (tools, guides, links).
- Add brief descriptions for each resource.
- Include tags or labels for filtering.
- Highlight top recommended resources in a callout section.
- End with a Quick Access Links section for key resources.

prompts.title: Learning Roadmap
prompts.prompt:
Transform this content into a structured learning roadmap.
- Begin with a clear learning goal at the top.
- Organize content into progressive skill levels (Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced).
- For each level, create bullet points of specific skills to master.
- Add resource recommendations in toggle blocks under each skill.
- Include milestone markers as callouts.
- End with a completion checklist to track progress.

prompts.title: Weekly Review Template
prompts.prompt:
Convert this text into a weekly review template.
- Start with a week overview callout (achievements, challenges).
- Create H2 sections for major categories (Work, Personal, Health).
- Add progress meters for ongoing goals and habits.
- Include a lessons learned section with bullet points.
- End with a next week planning section with priority tasks.

prompts.title: Client Management Hub
prompts.prompt:
Transform this into a client management hub.
- Create a client overview section with key stats.
- Organize information into H2 sections by client or project.
- Include status indicators (Active, On Hold, Completed).
- Add communication logs in toggle blocks.
- Create a deliverables tracker with checkboxes.
- End with an upcoming deadlines callout.

prompts.title: Competitor Analysis
prompts.prompt:
Reformat this data into a structured competitor analysis.
- Begin with a market overview section.
- Create H2 sections for each competitor.
- Use tables to compare features, pricing, and strengths/weaknesses.
- Highlight competitive advantages in callout blocks.
- Add SWOT analysis for key competitors.
- End with strategic recommendations based on findings.

prompts.title: Product Launch Planner
prompts.prompt:
Convert this into a product launch planner.
- Begin with a product vision callout.
- Organize tasks into phase-based H2 sections (Pre-launch, Launch, Post-launch).
- Create a timeline with key milestones.
- Add team responsibilities in a table format.
- Include risk assessment toggle blocks.
- End with a success metrics section to track performance.

prompts.title: Concept Map
prompts.prompt:
Restructure this information into a concept mapping format.
- Start with a central idea or theme at the top.
- Organize related concepts into distinct H2 sections.
- Use indented bullet points to show hierarchical relationships.
- Highlight key connections between concepts in callout blocks.
- Add examples in toggle blocks under each concept.
- Create a terminology section for important definitions.
- End with synthesis questions that connect multiple concepts.

prompts.title: Documentation Template
prompts.prompt:
Convert this into a structured documentation page.
- Begin with a purpose statement explaining what this document covers.
- Include a table of contents with links to major sections.
- Organize content into logical H2/H3 sections (Overview, Setup, Usage, etc.)
- Use code blocks for any technical instructions or examples.
- Add callout blocks for warnings, tips, or important notes.
- Include troubleshooting FAQs in a toggle section.
- End with related resources and where to get additional help.

prompts.title: Book Notes Template
prompts.prompt:
Restructure this into a book notes template.
- Start with a book summary callout (title, author, genre, publication date).
- Create H2 sections for key themes or chapters.
- Extract important quotes into toggle blocks.
- Add a personal reflections section.
- Include a vocabulary list for new terms.
- End with actionable takeaways in a bulleted list.

prompts.title: Recipe Collection
prompts.prompt:
Reformat this into a structured recipe collection.
- Group recipes into meal type categories (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Snacks).
- For each recipe, create a toggle with:
  - Ingredients list as a checklist
  - Step-by-step instructions as numbered list
  - Prep time & cooking time in a callout
- Add a favorites section at the top.
- Include dietary tags (vegetarian, gluten-free, etc.) for easy reference.

prompts.title: Travel Itinerary
prompts.prompt:
Convert this into a detailed travel itinerary.
- Begin with a trip overview (dates, destination, purpose).
- Organize by days as H2 headers with dates.
- For each day, create a timeline of activities and transport.
- Add accommodation details in callout blocks.
- Include packing list as a checklist.
- Create a budget tracker section.
- End with emergency contacts and important information.

prompts.title: Habit Tracker
prompts.prompt:
Transform this into a habit tracking system.
- Start with a habit overview (what and why).
- Create a progress calendar layout.
- Add milestone markers as callouts.
- Include reflection prompts in toggle blocks.
- Create a rewards system checklist.
- End with a strategies for consistency section.

prompts.title: Workshop Facilitator Guide
prompts.prompt:
Reformat this into a workshop facilitator guide.
- Start with a workshop overview (objectives, audience, duration).
- Create a minute-by-minute agenda as H2 sections.
- Include activity instructions in toggle blocks.
- Add discussion questions in callout blocks.
- Create a materials checklist.
- End with follow-up actions and evaluation methods.

libraries.name: Workspace Cleanup Suite
libraries.description: 10 systematic audit prompts for Notion workspace maintenance.

prompts.title: Duplicate Page Finder
prompts.prompt:
Search this workspace for pages that appear to cover the same topic, person, or project more than once. Look for:
- Pages with nearly identical titles
- Pages with the same TypeCode prefix pointing to the same subject
- Database entries that describe the same entity under slightly different names

For each duplicate cluster you find:
1. List all the pages in the cluster with their URLs and parent locations
2. Identify which one appears most recently edited or most complete
3. Recommend: Keep / Archive / Merge — with one sentence of reasoning

Do not delete anything. Output a table with columns: Cluster | Page Name | URL | Parent | Recommended Action | Reasoning

prompts.title: Naming Standard Violations Audit
prompts.prompt:
Audit the pages in this section of the workspace against the following naming rules:
- Page titles should begin with a TypeCode prefix (e.g. GOV, HUB, DOC, SOP, REF, PRJ, SFT, EVT, NOTE, LOG, DB, OPS, TEMP)
- After the TypeCode, titles should follow the pattern: [TypeCode] | [Descriptive Name] - [Date if applicable]
- Hub pages should be prefixed HUB |
- Database pages should be prefixed DB |

For each page that violates these rules:
1. List the current title and URL
2. Suggest a corrected title following the naming standard
3. Flag if the page appears to be a draft, duplicate, or orphan

Output a table: Current Title | URL | Suggested Title | Flag (Draft / Duplicate / Orphan / OK)

prompts.title: Orphan Page Detector
prompts.prompt:
Search for pages in this section that:
- Are not linked or mentioned from any other page
- Have no parent database entry pointing to them
- Have not been edited in more than 60 days
- Have no content other than a title

For each orphan page found:
1. List the title, URL, and last edited date
2. Estimate its likely original purpose based on the title
3. Recommend: Keep and link | Archive | Delete (if clearly empty)

Do not delete anything. Output a table: Title | URL | Last Edited | Likely Purpose | Recommended Action

prompts.title: TEMP and Draft Page Sweep
prompts.prompt:
Search this workspace for pages that appear to be temporary, draft, or staging in nature. Look for:
- Pages prefixed TEMP |
- Pages in folders or sections labeled Archive, Needs Review, Staging, or Inbox
- Pages whose titles contain words like "draft," "copy," "old," "backup," "test," or "untitled"
- Pages created more than 90 days ago that have never been linked from another page

For each page found:
1. List the title, URL, parent location, and created date
2. Identify if it has any content worth keeping
3. Recommend: Promote (move to canonical location) | Archive | Delete

Output a table: Title | URL | Parent | Created | Has Content | Recommended Action

prompts.title: Hub Page Navigation Audit
prompts.prompt:
Audit this hub page for navigation quality. Check for:
- Links that point to pages that no longer exist or have been moved
- Linked views that are pointing to the wrong database or show no results
- Sections that reference pages not actually related to this hub's topic
- Missing links to pages that clearly belong here but are not currently linked

For each issue found:
1. Describe the problem
2. Identify the specific element (link, section, view, callout)
3. Recommend the fix

Do not edit the page. Output a numbered list of issues with recommendations.

prompts.title: Database Schema Cleanup
prompts.prompt:
Audit this database schema for cleanup opportunities. Look for:
- Properties with names like "Text," "Text (1)," "Text 1," or other clearly placeholder names
- Properties that appear to duplicate another property's purpose
- Properties with no values across any entries
- Select or multi-select properties whose option lists have grown inconsistent

For each issue found:
1. Name the property and describe the problem
2. Recommend: Rename | Delete | Merge with [other property] | Leave as-is
3. If renaming, suggest the correct name

Output a table: Property Name | Issue | Recommended Action | Notes

prompts.title: Projects Stale Entry Sweep
prompts.prompt:
Review the entries in DB | Projects and flag any that need attention. Look for:
- Entries with Status = Doing or Ongoing but no Next Action filled in
- Entries with Status = Doing but Last Update older than 30 days
- Entries with no Category assigned
- Entries with no Priority assigned

For each flagged entry:
1. List the project name and URL
2. Describe the specific issue
3. Suggest the corrective action

Output a table: Project Name | URL | Issue | Suggested Action

prompts.title: Inbox Zero Sweep
prompts.prompt:
Review all items currently in this inbox or staging area. For each item:
1. Read the title and any available content
2. Determine the most likely destination based on the workspace structure
3. For each item, output: Title | Current Location | Recommended Destination | Action (Move / Archive / Convert to task)

Do not move anything. Output the full triage table first for review.

prompts.title: Cross-System Link Consistency Check
prompts.prompt:
Check cross-linking consistency across the main hub pages in this workspace. For each hub:
1. List which of the other hubs it currently links to
2. Identify any cross-links that are missing and should exist
3. Flag any links that point to outdated or moved pages

Output a matrix table: Hub | Currently Links To | Missing Links | Broken Links

prompts.title: Post-Session Cleanup
prompts.prompt:
This workspace just went through a significant build or working session. Help me do a post-session cleanup sweep. Search for:
- Pages created in the last 7 days that have no parent hub link and are not in a database
- Pages created in the last 7 days with placeholder titles (Untitled, Copy of, Test, Draft)
- Any open to-do checkboxes that are more than 3 days old

For each item found:
1. List the title, URL, and created date
2. Recommend the next action: File | Link to hub | Fill in Next Action | Process from inbox | Close out

Output a grouped list by category.

libraries.name: Document & Process Formatter
libraries.description: Prompts for turning raw content into professional reports, SOPs, proposals, and polished documents.

prompts.title: Task Steps
prompts.prompt: Transform the data I provide into a step-by-step checklist. Each item should be a concise, action-oriented task. Preserve the original order where it makes sense, and group related tasks under short headings.

prompts.title: Doc Rewrite
prompts.prompt: Take the data I provide and rewrite it as clear, user-friendly documentation. Use short sections with headings, define any important terms, and keep each sentence simple and unambiguous.

prompts.title: Pro Report
prompts.prompt: Format this text into a professional report with a title, introduction, 3–5 main sections with headings, and a brief closing section. Do not change any factual content.

prompts.title: Polish Doc
prompts.prompt: Take this messy draft and rewrite it as a polished document with clear headings, short paragraphs, and consistent spacing. Keep my voice, but improve clarity and flow.

prompts.title: SOP Format
prompts.prompt: Restructure this text as a standard operating procedure (SOP) with sections for purpose, scope, prerequisites, step-by-step instructions, and troubleshooting tips.

prompts.title: Meet Notes
prompts.prompt: Turn this content into a clean meeting note: add a title, date, attendees, agenda, decisions made, and action items sections, and sort information into the right section.

prompts.title: Proposal FX
prompts.prompt: Format this content as a client-facing proposal with a cover section, problem statement, proposed solution, timeline, and pricing or next steps section.

prompts.title: Idea Outline
prompts.prompt: Convert this rough brainstorm into a well-organized outline with hierarchical headings, bullet points, and logical grouping of related ideas.

prompts.title: KB Article
prompts.prompt: Turn this text into a structured knowledge base article with sections for overview, key concepts, examples, FAQs, and links or references if mentioned.

prompts.title: Skim Ready
prompts.prompt: Take this long block of text and reformat it into a skimmable document: add headings, bullet points, numbered lists, and emphasis where appropriate to improve readability.

prompts.title: Proj Brief
prompts.prompt: Reformat this into a project brief with sections for objective, background, deliverables, timeline, stakeholders, and risks or constraints.

libraries.name: Information Tracking & Ops
libraries.description: Prompts for extracting, organizing, and monitoring ongoing information.

prompts.title: Track Info
prompts.prompt: Extract all key information from the data I provide and organize it into a tracking format with clear fields such as item name, status, date, owner, and notes. Flag any incomplete or missing information.

prompts.title: Log Parse
prompts.prompt: Take the log or record I provide and convert it into a structured tracking table with columns for timestamp, event type, description, and any relevant metadata. Sort entries chronologically.

prompts.title: Status Board
prompts.prompt: From the information I provide, create a status dashboard layout showing current state, progress indicators, last updated date, and next actions for each tracked item.

prompts.title: Change Log
prompts.prompt: Convert the data I provide into a change log format with entries for date, version/iteration, what changed, who made the change, and reason for change where applicable.

prompts.title: Issue Track
prompts.prompt: Structure the information I provide as an issue tracker with fields for issue ID, title, priority, status, assigned to, date reported, and resolution notes.

libraries.name: Prompt Engineering Meta-Toolkit
libraries.description: A prompt pack for working ON prompts — diagnostic scoring, intent extraction, and prompt hardening.

prompts.title: Prompt Intent Extractor
prompts.prompt:
Analyze the following prompt and identify its true intent.

Prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Return:
- Primary task
- Secondary implied tasks
- Requested artifact
- Likely output medium
- Tone signals
- Formatting signals
- Ambiguities
- Missing constraints
- What the model is most likely to infer on its own

prompts.title: Seed Prompt Diagnostic
prompts.prompt:
You are a prompt diagnostics analyst.

Evaluate this prompt as a seed prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Score each from 1 to 5 and explain briefly:
- Execution Strength
- Artifact Specificity
- Medium Clarity
- Structure Control
- Audience Control
- Tone Control
- Constraint Coverage
- Ambiguity Load
- Enhancement Yield
- Assumption Risk

Then provide:
- Overall diagnosis in one sentence
- Main strengths
- Main weaknesses
- What a prompt enhancement layer should add carefully

prompts.title: Prompt Decomposition
prompts.prompt:
Break down the following prompt into its functional parts.

Prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Identify:
- Role or persona instructions
- Core task directive
- Output format instructions
- Constraints
- Tone or style guidance
- Audience signals
- Hidden assumptions
- Risky ambiguities

Then rewrite the breakdown as a compact table.

prompts.title: Enhancement Risk Review
prompts.prompt:
Review this prompt and determine how risky it would be for an enhancement system to expand it.

Prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Assess:
- Which details are explicit
- Which details would need to be inferred
- Which inferences are safe
- Which inferences could distort intent
- Whether the prompt should be expanded minimally, moderately, or aggressively

Then give:
- Risk level: low, medium, or high
- Recommended enhancement strategy

prompts.title: Original vs Enhanced Comparison
prompts.prompt:
Compare these two prompts.

Original prompt:
[PASTE ORIGINAL]

Enhanced prompt:
[PASTE ENHANCED]

Evaluate:
- What the enhanced version clarified
- What it preserved from the original
- What assumptions it introduced
- Whether it oversteered
- Whether the enhancement improved execution reliability

Return:
- A side by side score table
- A short verdict
- A safer improved version if needed

prompts.title: Medium Detection
prompts.prompt:
Determine what output medium this prompt most likely intends.

Prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Choose the best fit and explain why:
- Notion page
- Markdown article
- HTML page
- Email
- Report
- Slide deck
- Internal note
- Social post
- Other

Then state what wording in the prompt created the signal, and what wording is missing.

prompts.title: Ambiguity Extraction
prompts.prompt:
Interrogate this prompt for ambiguity.

Prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

List every phrase that is vague, subjective, or under-specified.
For each one, explain:
- Why it is ambiguous
- What a model might assume it means
- What risk that creates
- How to rewrite it more operationally

prompts.title: Constraint Recovery
prompts.prompt:
This prompt is under-specified. Recover the missing constraints without changing the likely intent.

Prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Infer the most important missing dimensions:
- audience
- tone
- length
- medium
- structure
- formatting
- exclusions
- output rules

For each:
- state whether it is explicit, implied, or missing
- suggest a cautious default

Then produce a minimally enhanced rewrite.

prompts.title: Prompt Hardening
prompts.prompt:
Take this prompt and harden it for more reliable execution while preserving its original meaning.

Prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Rules:
- preserve the user's intent
- do not over-template
- add only the minimum needed clarity
- make execution more deterministic

Return:
- revised prompt
- list of changes made
- explanation of why each change improves reliability

prompts.title: PromptLab Evaluator
prompts.prompt:
You are evaluating a prompt enhancement pipeline.

Seed prompt:
[PASTE SEED]

Enhanced prompt:
[PASTE ENHANCED]

Score 1 to 5:
- Intent Preservation
- Clarity Gain
- Execution Reliability
- Assumption Load
- Medium Accuracy
- Structural Helpfulness
- Oversteer Risk

Then answer:
- What did the enhancer do well?
- Where did it add too much?
- What would a better enhancement look like?
- Final verdict: good enhancement, acceptable but risky, or oversteered

prompts.title: Fast One-Shot Prompt Audit
prompts.prompt:
Audit this prompt quickly.

Prompt:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Return only:
- Prompt type
- Primary intent
- Biggest ambiguity
- Biggest strength
- Biggest risk
- Best minimal rewrite

prompts.title: Codex / Claude Code Feedback
prompts.prompt:
You are reviewing prompt quality for a development workflow.

Prompt under review:
[PASTE PROMPT]

Task:
Provide feedback suitable for Claude Code or Codex.

Include:
- one sentence diagnosis
- rubric scores from 1 to 5
- top three issues
- top three strengths
- recommended rewrite
- implementation advice for a prompt enhancement system

Keep the output compact, technical, and actionable.

libraries.name: Automotive Systems Research
libraries.description: Deep-dive research prompt for generating engineer-level technical reports on vehicle systems.

prompts.title: Full Vehicle Systems Research Report
prompts.prompt:
Produce a detailed, structured technical report explaining how the engine and all critical systems operate in a [Year] [Make] [Model] [Trim]. Treat this as a research assignment written for a technician who wants a clear, accurate, and mechanically grounded understanding of how their vehicle functions.

Your report must include these sections:

1. Engine Architecture Overview
2. Fuel System
3. Ignition System
4. Air Management & Emissions
5. Cooling System
6. Lubrication System
7. Transmission Integration
8. Electrical & Network Systems
9. Sensor Suite & ECU Logic
10. Critical Failure Modes
11. Maintenance Logic

Format: Clear headings, technical depth with accessible language, real-world failure examples. Keep all explanations specific to the vehicle.

libraries.name: AI Workflow Automation
libraries.description: System-level prompts for transcript extraction, page classification, and key topic generation.

prompts.title: Transcript Extraction & Organization
prompts.prompt:
Extract and organize this meeting/conversation transcript into a structured, loggable format with the following required fields:

1. Meeting Name/Title
2. Date & Time
3. Attendees & Roles
4. Parties Mentioned
5. Meeting Type/Format
6. Purpose & Objectives
7. Summary/Overview (4-6 sentences)
8. Detailed Key Topics/Agenda Items
9. Decisions Made
10. Open Issues/Follow-up
11. Supporting Materials & References
12. Notable Quotes/Memorable Statements
13. Sentiment & Dynamics
14. Additional Observations

prompts.title: Project Key Topics Generator
prompts.prompt:
Read the Project Name and Description fields provided. Extract 3-6 short key topics (max 3 words each) focusing on:
- Deliverables
- Goals
- Challenges
- Stakeholders
- Relevant tools

Output as a comma-separated list.

prompts.title: Notion Page Classification
prompts.prompt:
Analyze this page and determine the appropriate category/hub for organization.

Categories:
- Personal HQ
- Finance & Tasks
- Work HQ
- Shared & Collab
- Tech & AI
- Workflow & Organization
- Templates & Resources
- Archive

Output format:
Category: [Category Name]
Subcategory: [Specific Section]
Reasoning: [Brief explanation]

libraries.name: Validated Patch Workflows
libraries.description: Reusable prompts for scoped patch passes, prototype stability fixes, and single-file widget hardening.

prompts.title: Validated Patch Pass
prompts.prompt:
You are applying a validated patch to an existing codebase.

Rules:
- inspect the relevant files first
- preserve current behavior unless the requested fix requires a behavior change
- make the smallest viable safe edits
- do not refactor unrelated code
- keep naming and architecture consistent with the target project
- validate the changed path with the lightest useful check available

Execution:
1. Restate the target issue in one sentence.
2. Identify the exact files and functions involved.
3. Apply the patch directly.
4. Verify the change with code-level or runtime-level checks.
5. Return:
   - summary
   - files touched
   - exact changes
   - why safe
   - validation checklist
   - follow-up issues not touched

If the repo contains dirty unrelated work, avoid those files unless the task explicitly requires them.

prompts.title: Prototype P1 Stability Sweep
prompts.prompt:
You are doing a P1 stability pass on a prototype UI file.

Constraints:
- smallest safe change set only
- preserve design language
- no framework migration
- no dependency changes
- no unrelated cleanup

Prioritize these P1 fixes:
1. Reveal and animation safety: no hidden content if IntersectionObserver is missing, and respect reduced-motion preferences.
2. Scroll/nav stability: passive listeners where appropriate, initial active-state sync, cleanup of pending animation frames or listeners.
3. DOM side-effect cleanup: remove injected styles, links, or timers on unmount when the component owns them.
4. Keyboard and landmark truth: skip link targets a real main landmark, active nav state is exposed with aria semantics.
5. Mobile navigation baseline: ensure the nav remains usable at narrow widths; optionally upgrade to a hamburger menu if that is the cleanest fix.

Return:
- summary
- files touched
- exact P1 fixes applied
- how each was verified
- residual risks

prompts.title: Single-File Widget Hardening
prompts.prompt:
Harden this self-contained HTML widget without changing its product contract.

Constraints:
- single-file HTML only
- no external JS or CSS dependencies
- preserve URL parameter schema and legacy aliases unless explicitly told otherwise
- render user content safely with DOM APIs
- smallest viable safe changes only

Audit and patch for:
- reduced-motion handling
- ARIA state parity for interactive controls
- live-region semantics for transient success/error states
- parameter parsing and token validation drift
- predictable embed behavior at narrow iframe widths

Return:
- summary
- files touched
- exact changes
- why safe
- validation checklist
- follow-up items still open
